input.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

raw.headline: Kids do not want to go to child-free weddings either

winner: At the child-free wedding, when a toddler snuck in and started eating cake, the guests all gasped—until they realized the kid was the *only* one having fun.

candidates.text: Why did the kid bring a ladder to the wedding? Because he heard there was no place for children... *literally*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with 'literally' adds clever double meaning, concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: I told my kid she can't come to my wedding—she said that's fine because she doesn't like *adult* parties anyway.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: Kids these days are just as picky about wedding invitations as their parents. "Child-free zone"? They’d probably prefer a "kid-free zone" next to the open bar.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the phrase 'child-free zone,' specific to kids' preferences, concise, and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: At the child-free wedding, when a toddler snuck in and started eating cake, the guests all gasped—until they realized the kid was the *only* one having fun.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: Kids these days—no ring, no wedding, just a bunch of them texting under the table about why they’d rather be at a kid’s birthday party where the cake is definitely bigger than any adult’s ego.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the topic with specific, relatable imagery and clever wordplay."}
